fact_nuggets.text: Pixilate (Japanese: Fairy Skin) is an Ability introduced in Generation VI. From Generation VIII onward, it is the signature Ability of Sylveon.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S MD Normal-type moves that the Pokemon uses become Fairy type and deal more damage! MD RT DX Normal-type moves used by the Pokemon become Fairy type and deal more damage.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation VI 1.1.2 Generation VII 1.1.3 Generation VIII 1.1.4 Affected moves 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation VI [ edit | edit source ] Pixilate causes all Normal-type moves used by the Pokemon to become Fairy-type and receive a 30% power boost. Electrify is applied before Pixilate, so if the Pokemon is affected by Electrify the move becomes Electric-type and does not have its power boosted by Pixilate. The type-changing effect is applied before Ion Deluge , so if both effects are applying, an originally Normal-type move will end up as a Fairy-type move (as it is no longer Normal-type when Ion Deluge would be applied). Generation VII [ edit | edit source ] Affected moves now receive a 20% power boost. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] If Max Strike is selected with Pixilate, it will turn into Max Starfall when used, unless the user is Gigantamax Alcremie , in which case it will instead turn into G-Max Finale . Affected moves [ edit | edit source ] The table below lists the moves affected by Pixilate, as well as the Pokemon that can have Pixilate and learn those moves. Move Type Cat. Power and Boost Learned by Body Slam Normal Physical 85 102 Breakneck Blitz Normal Physical Breakneck Blitz Normal Special Covet Normal Physical 60 72 Cut Normal Physical 50 60 Double-Edge Normal Physical 120 144 Echoed Voice Normal Special 40 48 Facade Normal Physical 70 84 False Swipe Normal Physical 40 48 Flail Normal Physical Frustration Normal Physical Fury Attack Normal Physical 15 18 Giga Impact Normal Physical 150 180 Headbutt Normal Physical 70 84 Hyper Beam Normal Special 150 180 Hyper Voice Normal Special 90 108 Last Resort Normal Physical 140 168 Pay Day Normal Physical 40 48 Quick Attack Normal Physical 40 48 Rage Normal Physical 20 24 Retaliate Normal Physical 70 84 Return Normal Physical Round Normal Special 60 72 Secret Power Normal Physical 70 84 Skull Bash Normal Physical 130 156 Snore Normal Special 50 60 Swift Normal Special 60 72 Tackle Normal Physical 40 48 Take Down Normal Physical 90 108 Tera Blast Normal Special 80 96 Trump Card Normal Special Uproar Normal Special 90 108 Weather Ball Normal Special 50 60 Although the following moves are affected by the Ability, they currently cannot be learned by any Pokemon with Pixilate. They may, however, be called via Metronome , Mirror Move , Copycat , or Mimic , or be used by a Pokemon that gained Pixilate by an Ability like Trace or Receiver or a move such as Skill Swap or Role Play . Contents 1 In battle 1.1 Generation VI 1.2 Generation VII 1.3 Generation VIII 1.4 Affected moves In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation VI [ edit | edit source ] Pixilate causes all Normal-type moves used by the Pokemon to become Fairy-type and receive a 30% power boost. Electrify is applied before Pixilate, so if the Pokemon is affected by Electrify the move becomes Electric-type and does not have its power boosted by Pixilate. The type-changing effect is applied before Ion Deluge , so if both effects are applying, an originally Normal-type move will end up as a Fairy-type move (as it is no longer Normal-type when Ion Deluge would be applied). Generation VII [ edit | edit source ] Affected moves now receive a 20% power boost. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] If Max Strike is selected with Pixilate, it will turn into Max Starfall when used, unless the user is Gigantamax Alcremie , in which case it will instead turn into G-Max Finale .
